commentary_text: The sportsbook consensus assigns Senegal a 77.9% win probability versus the model's 60.9%—a 17-percentage-point gap favoring the favorite. This confidence gap is sharpest on the Senegal win; the market also compresses the draw and Iraq win probabilities below the model's estimates. The market's heightened conviction could reflect recent form, injury updates, or lineup changes that the model's last training data does not capture. Alternatively, real-money flows may simply price in available public information differently than the statistical baseline. The dashboard flags this divergence as a signal worth monitoring; neither source has inherent claim to accuracy until the match outcome is known.
